claims.claim: The desktop Capabilities/Tools screen has 19 toolsets (source tools.ts), not 14 as the README prose says.
claims.note: Confirmed: 19 distinct toolset keys in tools.ts (web, x_search, browser, terminal, file, code_execution, computer_use, vision, image_gen, video_gen, tts, skills, memory, session_search, clarify, delegation, cronjob, moa, todo/task-planning). The README at github.com/fathah/hermes-desktop says '14 toolsets — web, browser, terminal, file, code execution, vision, image gen, TTS, skills, memory, session search, clarify, delegation, MoA, and task planning' (which is actually 15 listed). Source file is authoritative; the '14' is a curated/undercounted README grouping. INV1 and INV2 correct; the '20/21' counts a WebFetch returned were miscounts including title/subtitle metadata.

claims.claim: The unofficial desktop Capabilities list includes X Search but does NOT include Spotify, Home Assistant, or a separate 'Video Analysis' toolset.
claims.note: X Search present ('Search posts and content on X (Twitter)'). Spotify ABSENT, Home Assistant ABSENT (Home Assistant appears only as a messaging gateway, not a toolset). Only Video Generation present ('Generate videos from text or image prompts'); no 'Video Analysis' entry. This contrasts with the OFFICIAL agent (next claim).

claims.claim: Spotify, Home Assistant, X Search, and Video Analysis ARE official Hermes Agent built-in toolsets (with the named tools).
claims.note: Confirmed in official tools-reference: spotify_playback/devices/queue/search/playlists/albums/library (Spotify OAuth); ha_call_service/get_state/list_entities/list_services (Home Assistant); x_search (XAI_API_KEY or xAI Grok OAuth); video_analyze AND video_generate both exist; computer_use is macOS-only (cua-driver). Total registry ~71 tools. KEY DISTINCTION: these are official-agent toolsets; the unofficial desktop GUI surfaces only a 19-toolset subset that omits Spotify/Home Assistant/video_analyze.

claims.claim: Skills exist to drive Claude Code CLI and Codex CLI ('Delegate coding to ...').
claims.note: Confirmed verbatim in autonomous-ai-agents category: claude-code = 'Delegate coding to Claude Code CLI (features, PRs).'; codex = 'Delegate coding to OpenAI Codex CLI (features, PRs).'; opencode = 'Delegate coding to OpenCode CLI (features, PR review).' The 4th sibling is hermes-agent = 'Configure, extend, or contribute to Hermes Agent' — NOT a 'hermes-cli' delegation skill. No skill literally named 'hermes-cli' exists. INV2's correction is right.

claims.claim: 'Claude Design' is a real skill — but it is an HTML-artifact design skill, NOT an Anthropic product.
claims.note: Confirmed: claude-design = 'Design one-off HTML artifacts (landing, deck, prototype).' It is a creative skill in the catalog, unrelated to any Anthropic offering. Likely to confuse end users.

claims.claim: Each model has its own per-model API key field in the desktop Models screen.
claims.note: Confirmed verbatim: field label 'API Key', description 'Stored as an environment variable. Picks the matching env key based on the URL, or CUSTOM_API_KEY otherwise.' Per-model API keys are REAL. Release v0.5.2 also added per-model api_key auto-population. User's observation correct.

claims.claim: The desktop has exactly 8 memory providers (in memory.ts).
claims.note: Confirmed 8 in the unofficial desktop: honcho, hindsight, mem0, retaindb, supermemory, holographic, openviking, byterover. NOTE: this is NOT '3'. The official Hermes Agent docs list 9 providers (adds Memori). README mentions 6 'discoverable'. So the count is surface-dependent: desktop UI source = 8, official agent = 9. INV1 (8) and INV3 (9 official) both correct for their respective surfaces; INV3's guess that the UI shows '~3 on fresh install' is unverified speculation.

claims.claim: The official Hermes Agent lists 9 memory providers (Honcho, OpenViking, Mem0, Hindsight, Holographic, RetainDB, ByteRover, Supermemory, Memori).
claims.note: Confirmed verbatim, 9 providers, only one active at a time alongside always-on built-in MEMORY.md/USER.md. The user's recollection of '3' is wrong for both surfaces (desktop=8, official=9); '3' is unverified.

claims.claim: There is NO 'context engine' selector in the unofficial desktop UI (it is an official-agent-only config concept).
claims.note: Confirmed absent in desktop memory.ts. Officially, context.engine is a single plugin selector defaulting to 'compressor' (built-in lossy summarization), with alternatives like 'lcm' (lossless) that are never auto-activated. The user's 'context engine (how many? 3)' does not map to any desktop count; '3' is unverified and likely an install-state artifact or conflation with official config.

claims.claim: Auto-compression defaults: threshold 0.50, target_ratio 0.20, protect_last_n 20, protect_first_n 3, enabled true.
claims.note: All confirmed verbatim in official docs: enabled: true, threshold: 0.50 (fires at 50% of context window), target_ratio: 0.20, protect_last_n: 20, protect_first_n: 3 (hardcoded). Context engine default = 'compressor'. Caveat: these are official-agent config.yaml defaults, NOT a desktop GUI panel — the unofficial desktop exposes no compression settings UI. The numbers the user cited are correct; the surface (GUI vs config) is the only nuance.

claims.claim: The desktop accent/theme names are Mono, Midnight, Rose, Amber, Cyberpunk, Slate.
claims.note: CONTRADICTED. The actual THEMES array in constants.ts is 12 syntax-style themes: Dark, Light, Dracula, Nord, One Dark, GitHub Dark, Monokai, Solarized Dark, Gruvbox Dark, Tokyo Night, GitHub Light, Solarized Light. None of Mono/Midnight/Rose/Amber/Cyberpunk/Slate/Ember appear. The names Mono/Slate/Cyberpunk belong to the OFFICIAL CLI SKIN namespace (see next claim); Midnight/Rose/Amber appear in NEITHER. INV1 correct (MISMATCH). INV3 is WRONG: it claimed desktop accents 'Midnight/Ember/Mono/Cyberpunk/Rose CONFIRMED' — that is not in the current source.

claims.claim: Official Hermes CLI built-in skins are: default, ares, mono, slate, daylight, warm-lightmode, poseidon, sisyphus, charizard.
claims.note: Confirmed verbatim list of 9 built-in skins. mono and slate ARE built-in skins. cyberpunk is NOT a built-in (it appears only as a custom-skin example elsewhere) — so INV2/INV3 listing cyberpunk among built-ins is slightly off. midnight/rose/amber/ember are NOT built-in skins either. These are CLI terminal skins, a different namespace from the desktop GUI's 12 syntax themes.

claims.claim: The Hermes desktop UI supports Japanese (日本語) as a real locale.
claims.note: Confirmed: README language line 'English · 简体中文 · 日本語 · Español (LATAM)'; full ja/ locale directory exists; Japanese localization landed in desktop v0.5.2; v0.5.6 added an IME/CJK Enter-key fix specifically for Japanese/Chinese input. Genuinely supported, not just docs.

claims.claim: In the official Hermes Agent, 'ja' (Japanese) is a distinct display.language entry, separate from 'zh-hant' (Traditional Chinese).
claims.note: Confirmed: display.language default 'en'; supported list includes both 'ja' (Japanese) AND 'zh-hant' (Traditional Chinese) as separate entries (full list: en, zh, zh-hant, ja, de, es, fr, tr, uk, af, ko, it, ga, pt, ru, hu). This refutes INV3's flagged user-confusion 'zh-Hant = Japanese' — they are different rows, both present. (Caveat: display.language translates UI chrome only, not the agent's reply language.)

claims.claim: Memory/profile budgets are implemented as character limits: memory_char_limit 2200 (~800 tokens), user_char_limit 1375 (~500 tokens).
claims.note: Confirmed verbatim: memory_char_limit: 2200 (~800 tokens), user_char_limit: 1375 (~500 tokens). The desktop memory.ts shows '{{count}} chars' capacity displays, consistent with character-based (not token-slider) budgets. INV1 and INV3 correct.

claims.claim: The desktop sidebar labels the tools screen 'Capabilities', soul as 'Persona', and agents as 'Profiles'; there is NO 'Artifacts' or 'Projects' sidebar item.
claims.note: Confirmed verbatim: tools->'Capabilities', soul->'Persona', agents->'Profiles'. Full nav: Chat, Sessions, Discover, Profiles, Office, Models, Providers, Skills, Persona, Memory, Capabilities, Schedules, Kanban, Gateway, Settings. No 'Artifacts' and no 'Projects' label. Confirms INV1's MISMATCH calls. (Note: official desktop docs do describe an 'Artifacts'/preview-rail concept, and media renders inline in chat, but there is no standalone Artifacts sidebar screen in this app's source.)

claims.claim: Per-model API key behavior, OAuth providers, and per-model reasoning-effort picker are real (release-confirmed).
claims.note: Confirmed across releases: v0.5.2 per-model api_key auto-populated; v0.5.0 in-app OAuth sign-in for ChatGPT/xAI Grok/Qwen/Gemini CLI/MiniMax; v0.5.8 reasoning effort picker + Ollama Cloud; v0.5.9 Atlas Cloud + AIML API providers, Rename Sessions. Consistent with INV1/INV2/INV3.

claims.claim: The desktop has 16 messaging gateways including Home Assistant (as a gateway), and Gateway is the messaging control surface (not a 'Messaging' view).
claims.note: Confirmed verbatim: '16 messaging gateways — Telegram, Discord, Slack, WhatsApp, Signal, Matrix, Mattermost, Email (IMAP/SMTP), SMS (Twilio/Vonage), iMessage (BlueBubbles), DingTalk, Feishu/Lark, WeCom, WeChat (iLink Bot), Webhooks, Home Assistant'. Sidebar item is 'Gateway'. Home Assistant appears here as a delivery gateway, separate from its official toolset role.

claims.claim: Image sending (attach/drag-drop/paste, auto-compress) is real in the desktop chat.
claims.note: Confirmed: v0.4.5 'Chat attachments — image and text-file attachments via click, drag-and-drop, and paste'; v0.5.0 right-click copy/paste + render agent-generated media in chat; v0.5.0 per-conversation context folder. Inbound image analysis handled by the vision toolset. INV1/INV3 correct.

claims.claim: Voice in the desktop is speech-to-text input in Chat (v0.5.5) plus a TTS toolset — there is NO dedicated 'Voice' settings tab with a TTS-model picker.
claims.note: Confirmed: v0.5.5 'Prompt Efficiency and Speech to Text in Chat' (voice input/transcription in chat). Text-to-Speech is toolset #11 in tools.ts, not a settings panel. No 'Voice' top-level Settings section and no TTS-model picker in the desktop source — settings.ts sections are Hermes Agent / Appearance / Privacy / Credential Pool. The user's 'Voice -> TTS model picker' tree is unverified for this app.

summary:
Verified ~20 load-bearing claims against primary sources (fathah/hermes-desktop source files + GitHub README/releases, and official Hermes Agent docs). Two products are in play and must be kept separate: the UNOFFICIAL desktop GUI (fathah/hermes-desktop, what the user is looking at) and the OFFICIAL Hermes Agent (NousResearch). Most claims hold; the biggest correction is the accent-color claim.

KEY VERDICTS:
- Toolset list: desktop source tools.ts has 19 toolsets (verified), not the README's '14'. X Search is present in the desktop; Spotify, Home Assistant, and Video Analysis are NOT desktop toolsets but ARE official-agent toolsets (verified in tools-reference.md, ~71 tools, spotify_*/ha_*/x_search/video_analyze all real). Home Assistant appears in the desktop only as 1 of 16 messaging gateways.
- 'Drive Claude Code/Codex CLI' skills: VERIFIED verbatim (claude-code, codex, opencode are 'Delegate coding to ...'). There is NO 'hermes-cli' delegation skill — the 4th sibling is 'hermes-agent' (self-config). 'Claude Design' is a real skill but is an HTML-artifact design skill, NOT an Anthropic product.
- Per-model API keys: VERIFIED REAL (models.ts: each model has its own 'API Key' field).
- Memory providers: NOT 3. Desktop source = 8 (verified in memory.ts); official agent = 9 (verified, adds Memori). The user's '3' is unverified/wrong on both surfaces.
- Context engine: NO selector in the desktop UI (verified absent). Officially it's a plugin defaulting to 'compressor'. The '3 context engines' count is unverified.
- Compression: threshold 0.50 / target_ratio 0.20 / protect_last_n 20 / protect_first_n 3 / enabled true — ALL VERIFIED, but they are official config.yaml defaults, not a desktop GUI panel.
- Accent colors: CONTRADICTED. The desktop's actual themes (constants.ts) are 12 syntax-style themes (Dark/Light/Dracula/Nord/One Dark/GitHub Dark/Monokai/Solarized Dark/Gruvbox Dark/Tokyo Night/GitHub Light/Solarized Light). Mono/Midnight/Rose/Amber/Cyberpunk/Slate are NOT in the desktop source. Mono/Slate are official CLI SKINS; Midnight/Rose/Amber/Ember appear in NEITHER namespace. INV3 was wrong here (it claimed Midnight/Ember/Mono/Cyberpunk/Rose were CONFIRMED desktop accents — they are not). Also note: 'cyberpunk' is NOT an official built-in skin (only a custom-skin example), so INV2/INV3 listing it among built-ins is slightly off.
- Japanese UI: VERIFIED. Desktop supports 日本語 (full ja/ locale, v0.5.2, plus a v0.5.6 CJK IME fix). Officially 'ja' is a distinct display.language entry, separate from 'zh-hant' (Traditional Chinese) — refuting the 'zh-Hant = Japanese' confusion INV3 flagged. Caveat: language setting changes UI chrome, not the agent's reply language.
- Sidebar labels VERIFIED (Capabilities/Persona/Profiles); no Artifacts or Projects sidebar screen. Image attach/drag-drop/paste VERIFIED. Voice = STT-in-chat + TTS toolset, no dedicated Voice settings tab.

BOTTOM LINE: The user is looking at the unofficial fathah/hermes-desktop. The single hard contradiction is the accent-color names (those belong to the official CLI skin set or to nowhere). The '3 memory providers' / '3 context engines' counts are wrong/unverified. Everything else — per-model keys, the CLI-delegation skills, Claude Design, Japanese support, compression numbers, the big toolset list (with the desktop-vs-official subset caveat) — checks out against primary sources.
